title: Tunisian Prawn Tagine
description: A traditional North African stew originating from Tunisia, this dish is a flavorful blend of aromatic spices and fresh seafood. Perfect for a romantic evening or a special occasion.

ingredients.name: 2 tablespoons olive oil
ingredients.notes: use a high-quality Tunisian olive oil for authentic flavor

ingredients.name: 1 tablespoon ground cumin
ingredients.notes: use a high-quality Tunisian cumin for authentic flavor

ingredients.name: 2 cups vegetable broth
ingredients.notes: use a low-sodium broth for a healthier option

instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large clay pot or heavy Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the onion and cook, stirring occasionally, until softened and lightly browned, about 8 minutes.
Add the garlic and cook, stirring constantly, for 1 minute.
Add the cumin, coriander, cinnamon, cayenne pepper, salt, and black pepper. Cook, stirring constantly, for 1 minute.
Add the prawns and cook, stirring occasionally, until pink and cooked through, about 3-4 minutes.
Add the vegetable broth and bring to a simmer.
Reduce the heat to low and cook, covered, until the prawns are cooked through and the sauce has thickened, about 10-12 minutes.
Stir in the chopped parsley and serve over couscous or rice.
Garnish with additional parsley and lemon wedges, if desired.
